title: dactylorhiza brennensis
intro: Dactylorhiza brennensis, also known as the Hungarian marsh orchid, is a type of orchid native to Central and Eastern Europe, characterized by its unique white and purple flower colors. The plant has been used in traditional medicine for its medicinal properties, which include anti-inflammatory and antiseptic properties due to its high content of iridoid glycosides and phenolic compounds. Dactylorhiza brennensis is believed to have several health benefits, including reducing inflammation, improving cardiovascular health, and boosting the immune system. The plant can be used in herbal preparations such as infusions, tinctures, and capsules, although its availability and usage may vary depending on regional regulations.
